content: Treatment involves surgery and chemotherapy. Early stage (I-II): surgical staging (TAH-BSO, omentectomy, lymph node dissection, peritoneal biopsies); adjuvant chemotherapy for stage IC-II (carboplatin + paclitaxel x6 cycles). Advanced stage (III-IV): primary debulking surgery aiming for R0 resection (no visible residual) followed by carboplatin + paclitaxel x6 cycles; or neoadjuvant chemotherapy x3-4 cycles, interval debulking, then additional chemotherapy (EORTC 55971, CHORUS trials showed equivalent OS). Intraperitoneal chemotherapy considered for optimally debulked stage III. Maintenance therapy: PARP inhibitors for BRCA-mutated (olaparib, niraparib, rucaparib) based on SOLO-1, PRIMA trials; bevacizumab for high-risk patients. Recurrent disease: platinum-sensitive (>6 months since last platinum) - platinum-based doublet + bevacizumab, then PARP inhibitor maintenance; platinum-resistant (<6 months) - single-agent non-platinum (liposomal doxorubicin, weekly paclitaxel, gemcitabine, topotecan) ± bevacizumab. FRα-positive patients: mirvetuximab soravtansine (SORAYA trial).
